seasons.title: The Last Ronaldinho Summer
seasons.dek: Ronaldinho’s Barcelona win Europe, Chelsea repeat in England, Juventus are swallowed by Calciopoli, and the 2006 World Cup closes Zidane’s career in chaos.

seasons.title: United Return, Milan Revenge
seasons.dek: Cristiano Ronaldo becomes a superstar in England, Manchester United take back the league, and Milan avenge Istanbul by beating Liverpool in Athens.

seasons.title: Ronaldo’s First Crown
seasons.dek: Cristiano Ronaldo wins everything with Manchester United, Spain finally begin their international dynasty, and English clubs dominate Europe.

seasons.title: Pep Changes the Game
seasons.dek: Guardiola’s Barcelona win the treble, Messi moves into the center of the story, and Manchester City’s takeover changes the Premier League’s future.

seasons.title: Inter Stop the Future
seasons.dek: Mourinho’s Inter win the treble, Barcelona and Spain still define the technical direction of football, and Real Madrid begin the new Galáctico project.

seasons.title: Barcelona’s Wembley Lesson
seasons.dek: Messi, Xavi and Iniesta deliver one of the defining Champions League final performances, Dortmund announce a new German wave, and Qatar’s arrival at PSG foreshadows a new power map.

seasons.title: Messi 73, Madrid 100
seasons.dek: Real Madrid produce a 100-point league season, Messi reaches a statistical peak, City win the Premier League on goal difference, Chelsea steal Europe, and Spain complete the international three-peat.

seasons.title: Bayern Complete the Arc
seasons.dek: Bayern avenge their home final defeat, Dortmund push gegenpressing into the mainstream, and PSG begin to look like a new European heavyweight.

seasons.title: La Décima Arrives
seasons.dek: Real Madrid finally win the tenth European Cup, Atlético break the Spain duopoly, Germany win in Brazil, and Suárez nearly drags Liverpool to the league.

seasons.title: MSN Treble
seasons.dek: Barcelona’s Messi-Suárez-Neymar front three win the treble, Chelsea return to the top in England, Juventus reach Berlin, and Ronaldo posts another huge scoring year.

seasons.title: Leicester Break Reality
seasons.dek: Leicester win the Premier League against impossible odds, Real Madrid begin another Champions League dynasty, Barcelona remain Spain’s dominant force, and Portugal lift Euro 2016 with Ronaldo watching from the sidelines.

seasons.title: Madrid Go Back-to-Back
seasons.dek: Real Madrid become the first Champions League repeat winners of the modern era, Monaco explode onto the scene behind a teenage Mbappé, Chelsea storm England under Conte, and Europe begins to shift toward a new generation.

seasons.title: Threepeat and Kiev
seasons.dek: Real Madrid complete the Champions League three-peat, Modrić reaches the Ballon d’Or summit after leading Croatia to the World Cup final, Salah transforms Liverpool, and Manchester City hit 100 points.

seasons.title: Liverpool Finish the Climb
seasons.dek: Liverpool win Europe after a 97-point league season, Ajax captivate the continent with a fearless young side, Manchester City complete a domestic treble, and Anfield hosts one of football’s greatest comebacks.

seasons.title: Pandemic and Bayern Machine
seasons.dek: Liverpool end the 30-year league wait, Bayern become European champions in a compressed Lisbon tournament, and the pandemic transforms the season’s rhythm.

seasons.title: Lille Break Paris
seasons.dek: Chelsea win Europe under Tuchel, Lille take Ligue 1 from PSG, Inter end Juventus’ Serie A streak, and Italy win Wembley on penalties.

seasons.title: Madrid Refuse to Die
seasons.dek: Real Madrid survive PSG, Chelsea and Manchester City on the way to another Champions League, City edge Liverpool again, and Messi leaves Barcelona for Paris.

seasons.title: City Complete the Mission
seasons.dek: Manchester City win the treble, Napoli take Serie A in style, Messi wins the World Cup, and Haaland changes Premier League scoring expectations.

seasons.title: Leverkusen Invincible
seasons.dek: Leverkusen go unbeaten domestically, Real Madrid win Europe again, Spain win Euro 2024, and the next generation begins to take over.

seasons.title: PSG Finally Arrive
seasons.dek: PSG win their first Champions League, Barcelona’s young core returns to the top in Spain, Liverpool move beyond Klopp, and Napoli reclaim Serie A under Conte.

seasons.title: PSG Retain, Arsenal Arrive
seasons.dek: Arsenal return to the top in England, PSG retain the Champions League on penalties, Barcelona hold Spain, Bayern run away with Germany, and the 2026 World Cup waits for the summer.

sourceNotes: Top-four league placements are entered as final standings for the five major leagues from 2005-06 through 2024-25. Serie A 2005-06 uses post-Calciopoli official placement, not the original table order.
Champions League finals are entered from UEFA historical final records.
Ballon d’Or entries use the award top-three by calendar year shown in that season chapter. The 2020 Ballon d’Or was canceled, so 2020-21 has no top-three list.
Golden Boot entries use European Golden Shoe winners and domestic league top scorers. Tied domestic top scorers are entered as separate rows.
Core players are curated for learning context. S tier means central identity/season-defining player; A tier means major core contributor. They are not exact statistical rankings.
International tournaments are attached to the season chapter that leads into or contains that tournament summer/window.
2025-26 includes completed club-season and Champions League results as of June 2026; the 2026 World Cup is marked Coming soon because it takes place in summer 2026.
